title: What are mRNA vaccines, and how do they work?
summary: mRNA vaccines train the immune system in a similar way to traditional vaccines, but they use a different strategy to get there.

title: Daniel De Carvalho wins 2025 Peter Gilgan Canada Gairdner Momentum Award
summary: De Carvalho, an associate professor of medical biophysics at the U of T’s Temerty Faculty of Medicine, was recognized for his impactful contributions to cancer epigenetics.

title: New blood test method improves accuracy of cancer detection
summary: A new, error-corrected method for detecting cancer from blood samples is much more sensitive and accurate than prior methods and may be useful for monitoring disease status in patients following treatment, according to a study by Weill Cornell Medicine and New York Genome Center investigators.

title: Being married linked to increased risk of dementia - new study
summary: Large study questions marriage’s protective effect on brain health. But the picture is complex.

title: Neighborhood disadvantage tied to higher breast cancer mortality in black women
summary: A new study finds that Black women living in economically disadvantaged neighborhoods face significantly higher breast cancer mortality rates.

title: Brains of people with schizophrenia may age faster - how our research adds to the evidence
summary: Study suggests accelerated brain ageing is a factor in the development of schizophrenia – and can be measured using a simple blood test.

title: Key genes in Parkinson's disease discovered using CRISPR technology
summary: A longstanding mystery in Parkinson's disease research has been why some individuals carrying pathogenic variants that increase their risk of PD go on to develop the disease, while others who also carry such variants do not. The prevailing theory has suggested additional genetic factors may play a role.

title: Common Causes of Pregnancy Loss in Horses
summary: A theriogenologist describes risk factors for abortion at all stages of a mare’s gestation.

title: To eradicate polio once and for all, we need a new vaccine - that’s what we’re working on
summary: Researchers are using virus-like particles to develop a safer, cheaper polio vaccine to help eradicate the disease once and for all.

title: Your New Acne Skincare Routine, According to the Pros
summary: Pimple-prone skin? Dermatologists and aestheticians share tips for the ultimate acne skincare routine—including cleansers, exfoliants, tools, and more.
